title: Monetary Policy Report—January 2026
summary: The Bank of Canada's January 2026 Monetary Policy Report indicates the Canadian economy is undergoing adjustment to US tariffs and evolving global trade conditions. Growth projections remain modest as this adjustment process unfolds over time. The central bank projects inflation to stay close to its 2% target.
key_points: Canadian economy adjusting to US tariffs and new global trade landscape
Growth expected to be modest during adjustment period
Inflation projected to remain near the 2% target
Adjustment process expected to take time
why_it_matters: This report signals the Bank of Canada's outlook on how trade policy uncertainty may dampen Canadian economic growth while maintaining inflation near target, suggesting a potential holding pattern for monetary policy.
macro_context: US tariff implementation creating trade headwinds for Canada; central bank balancing modest growth outlook with stable inflation expectations.
